results.identified_issue: Modeled the closing screen as a new DiscoverPage.end appended to the feed only when the question has clips, so it inherits the existing lock/unlock/auto-advance machinery for free — the last argument finishing unlocks and rolls onto it just like any other page. The 'next question' button reuses select(completingCurrent:true) and a randomNextQuestionID() that prefers unheard questions with answers, falling back to any other question. .end has a fixed sentinel UUID (safe because only one question's feed exists at a time). Alternative considered: opening the Browse sheet, which the reporter explicitly retracted mid-sentence in favor of a random jump. Not yet run on device (skill forbids building); verified with swiftc -parse only.
results.summary: Added an 'end of answers' screen to Discover with a button that jumps to a random next question
results.detailed_description: The Discover feed used to just stop dead on the last argument of a question. Now, once you've heard the final clip, the feed rolls onto a new closing screen — 'You've heard both sides / That's every argument on this question' — with a prominent 'Next question' button. Tapping it marks the current question as heard and drops you onto a fresh question chosen at random, preferring questions that have answers and that you haven't been through yet, so it keeps surfacing new arguments before it repeats. If the library has no other question to offer, the screen says so instead of showing a dead button. The closing screen only appears on questions that actually have answers (a question with no answers keeps its lone card). It reuses the feed's existing 'earn your way forward' locking, so you can't skip to it — the last argument has to play out first, after which it auto-advances onto the end screen hands-free. In the voice note you started to say the button should open the question sheet and then corrected yourself to 'just take us to a random next question,' so that's what the button does. Verified the file parses cleanly; I did not build or run it on device per the workflow, so please confirm the screen looks and behaves right on the phone.

description: We should have a final screen that's end of answers, and then it should have a button that says, that opens the question sheet, or actually it should just take us to a random question, a random next question.
